section_title: When the pawnor has obtained possession of the goods pledged by
content:
178A. When the pawnor has obtained possession of the goods pledged by
person in
him under a contract voidable under section 19 or section 19A, but the
possession
under contract has not been rescinded at the time of the pledge, the pawnee
voidable
acquires a good title to the goods, provided he acts in good faith and without
contract
notice of the pawnor's defect of title.]
Bailments Of Pledges
Pledge by
14[178. Where a mercantile agent is, with the consent of the owner, in
mercantile
possession of goods or the documents of title to goods, any pledge
agent
made by him, when acting in the ordinary course of business of a
mercantile agent, shall be as valid as if he were expressly authorized by
the owner of the goods to make the same; provided that the pawnee
acts in good faith and has not at the time of the pledge notice that the
pawnor has not authority to pledge.
Explanation – In this section, the expressions 'mercantile agent' and
'documents of title' shall have the meanings assigned to them in the Sale
of Goods Act, 1930.
